articles.title: Delhi-NCR Weather Update: Rain, thunderstorms lash city; sudden weather shift brings major relief from scorching heat, IMD issues travel advisory – Check here - news24online.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Chandigarh
articles.full_text:
New Delhi: The national capital’s weather changed suddenly on Friday, March 20, 2026. Dark clouds covered the sky around 5 am, and heavy rain started along with thunder and strong winds. While the rain brought relief from the heat, it also made commuting difficult for people leaving home early.
⚠️ IMD Weather Warning !
— India Meteorological Department (@Indiametdept) March 19, 2026
Scattered thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds, and heavy rain/hail are set to continue 🌧️
📍 Western Himalayan Region & Northwest plains: Till March 20
📍 Central & East India: Till March 22
Stay cautious and weather-ready! 🌪️#IMDWeatherWarning… pic.twitter.com/hhZ1IrfJau
Delhi-NCR weather today
Rain has continued for days in Delhi-NCR. Today, expect mostly cloudy skies. Light rain with thunderstorms and winds of 30-40 kmph (gusts up to 50 kmph) in the morning. More light rain, thunder, and gusty winds are possible till evening.
Delhi-NCR weather forecast for the coming days
On March 21, 2026, expect partly cloudy skies, with a maximum temperature of 28-30°C (below normal by 1.6-3.0°C) and a minimum of 14-16°C (near normal, varying by -1.5°C to 1.5°C). March 22 will also feature partly cloudy conditions, with no rain currently forecasted.
Heavy rain alerts issued for North India regions
IMD predicts heavy rain and snow in Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand. Rain warnings also for Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, western and eastern Uttar Pradesh, and eastern Rajasthan.
